exerciseTypes.name: Global comprehension (gist reading)
exerciseTypes.description: Read a text and answer a general question about its topic or purpose.

exerciseTypes.name: Detailed comprehension
exerciseTypes.description: Answer questions about specific information in the text (who, what, when, where).

exerciseTypes.name: True/False/Not stated
exerciseTypes.description: Evaluate statements against the content of a text as true, false, or not stated.

exerciseTypes.name: Matching headings to paragraphs
exerciseTypes.description: Match headings, summaries, or main ideas to sections of a text.

exerciseTypes.name: Ordering paragraphs/fragments
exerciseTypes.description: Reconstruct the logical order of a scrambled text.

exerciseTypes.name: Gap-fill in text (cloze)
exerciseTypes.description: Complete a text by filling in deleted words, with or without a word bank.

exerciseTypes.name: Inference and deduction
exerciseTypes.description: Answer questions whose responses are implicit rather than explicit in the text.

exerciseTypes.name: Register and intent identification
exerciseTypes.description: Determine the tone, register, target audience, or communicative intent of a text.

exerciseTypes.name: Critical reading / argued opinion
exerciseTypes.description: Read a text and formulate a reasoned opinion about its ideas.

exerciseTypes.name: True/False with justification
exerciseTypes.description: Evaluate statements about a text as true or false, quoting the text excerpt that justifies the answer.

exerciseTypes.name: Global listening
exerciseTypes.description: Listen to an audio recording and identify the general idea.

exerciseTypes.name: Selective listening
exerciseTypes.description: Extract specific data from an audio recording (names, numbers, dates, places).

exerciseTypes.name: Detailed listening
exerciseTypes.description: Answer comprehension questions about the full content of an audio recording.

exerciseTypes.name: Listen and complete
exerciseTypes.description: Fill in gaps in a partial transcript while listening.

exerciseTypes.name: Listen and take notes
exerciseTypes.description: Listen and take notes on key points independently.

exerciseTypes.name: Phonetic discrimination
exerciseTypes.description: Distinguish sounds, stress patterns, or minimal pairs.

exerciseTypes.name: Dictation (partial or complete)
exerciseTypes.description: Transcribe a spoken fragment, partially or in full.

exerciseTypes.name: Authentic media comprehension
exerciseTypes.description: Understand authentic media such as podcasts, news, interviews, or films without adaptation.

exerciseTypes.name: Guided writing with model
exerciseTypes.description: Write a text following a provided model by filling in a structure.

exerciseTypes.name: Complete sentences/paragraphs
exerciseTypes.description: Complete sentences or paragraphs with scaffolded prompts.

exerciseTypes.name: Short functional text
exerciseTypes.description: Write a short functional text such as a message, note, postcard, or form.

exerciseTypes.name: Description (person, place, object)
exerciseTypes.description: Produce a descriptive text about a person, place, or object following guidelines.

exerciseTypes.name: Narration
exerciseTypes.description: Write a story, anecdote, or sequence of events.

exerciseTypes.name: Letter/email (formal and informal)
exerciseTypes.description: Compose formal or informal correspondence with appropriate register.

exerciseTypes.name: Opinion/argumentative essay
exerciseTypes.description: Write a text expressing and justifying a personal stance.

exerciseTypes.name: Summary
exerciseTypes.description: Condense a source text into fewer words.

exerciseTypes.name: Register transformation
exerciseTypes.description: Rewrite a text changing its register (e.g., informal to formal).

exerciseTypes.name: Creative/literary text
exerciseTypes.description: Free creative production such as a poem, micro-story, diary entry, or chronicle.

exerciseTypes.name: Written mediation
exerciseTypes.description: Explain a complex text in simpler terms for a different audience.

exerciseTypes.name: Guided dialogue (closed role-play)
exerciseTypes.description: Act out a situation with concrete turn-by-turn instructions.

exerciseTypes.name: Open role-play
exerciseTypes.description: Participate in a communicative situation with a goal but no fixed script.

exerciseTypes.name: Structured interview
exerciseTypes.description: Conduct a structured interview: one student asks prepared questions, the other answers.

exerciseTypes.name: Oral description (image, experience)
exerciseTypes.description: Describe a photo, scene, person, or place orally.

exerciseTypes.name: Sustained monologue / presentation
exerciseTypes.description: Prepare and present a topic orally for 2 to 5 minutes.

exerciseTypes.name: Debate / discussion
exerciseTypes.description: Defend a position with arguments in a debate or discussion.

exerciseTypes.name: Negotiation / problem-solving
exerciseTypes.description: Negotiate and reach an agreement in a problem-solving scenario.

exerciseTypes.name: Thematic free conversation
exerciseTypes.description: Speak freely about a topic without a rigid structure, with teacher involvement.

exerciseTypes.name: Oral mediation
exerciseTypes.description: Explain, summarize, or paraphrase information for another person to understand.

exerciseTypes.name: Audio recording (oral homework)
exerciseTypes.description: Record a voice message describing, giving an opinion, or narrating.

exerciseTypes.name: Fill-in-the-blank
exerciseTypes.description: Complete sentences with the correct grammatical form.

exerciseTypes.name: Multiple choice
exerciseTypes.description: Choose the correct grammatical form from 3 to 4 options.

exerciseTypes.name: Sentence transformation
exerciseTypes.description: Rewrite a sentence changing one element (tense, person, voice).

exerciseTypes.name: Error correction
exerciseTypes.description: Identify and correct grammatical errors in sentences or texts.

exerciseTypes.name: Conjugation (paradigms)
exerciseTypes.description: Complete conjugation tables or write verb forms.

exerciseTypes.name: Matching
exerciseTypes.description: Match elements from two columns (form-function, sentence-image, etc.).

exerciseTypes.name: Word ordering (sentence building)
exerciseTypes.description: Reorder individual words to form a correct sentence.

exerciseTypes.name: Inductive discovery (noticing)
exerciseTypes.description: Observe examples and deduce the grammatical rule without drilling.

exerciseTypes.name: Classification/categorization
exerciseTypes.description: Classify grammatical forms into categories (e.g., regular/irregular, indicative/subjunctive).

exerciseTypes.name: Complete a paradigm from text
exerciseTypes.nameEs: Completar un paradigma desde el texto
exerciseTypes.description: Extract grammatical forms from a read text and organize them in a paradigm.

exerciseTypes.name: List with translation/definition
exerciseTypes.description: Present new vocabulary with its L1 equivalent or L2 definition.

exerciseTypes.name: Matching (word-definition, word-image)
exerciseTypes.description: Match words to their meanings or images.

exerciseTypes.name: Categorize/group by semantic field
exerciseTypes.description: Classify words into thematic categories.

exerciseTypes.name: Find in text (vocabulary in context)
exerciseTypes.description: Locate and deduce the meaning of unknown words in a text.

exerciseTypes.name: Complete sentences with vocabulary
exerciseTypes.description: Use words from a lexical field in contextualized sentences.

exerciseTypes.name: Synonyms and antonyms
exerciseTypes.description: Match or produce synonyms and antonyms.

exerciseTypes.name: Collocations
exerciseTypes.description: Match verbs, nouns, and adjectives that collocate naturally.

exerciseTypes.name: Mind map / lexical network
exerciseTypes.description: Organize vocabulary visually in a conceptual mind map.

exerciseTypes.name: Flashcards (spaced repetition)
exerciseTypes.description: Use flashcards with word, translation, and example for spaced review.

exerciseTypes.name: Idioms and fixed expressions
exerciseTypes.description: Present and practice idioms and fixed expressions.

exerciseTypes.name: Word formation (derivation, composition)
exerciseTypes.description: Work with prefixes, suffixes, and word families.

exerciseTypes.name: Communicative functions
exerciseTypes.description: Practice speech acts (requesting, apologizing, inviting, refusing) with appropriate register.

exerciseTypes.name: Cultural/intercultural content
exerciseTypes.description: Read, discuss, or investigate a cultural aspect of the Spanish-speaking world.

exerciseTypes.name: Intercultural comparison
exerciseTypes.description: Compare cultural practices of the Spanish-speaking world with the student's own.

exerciseTypes.name: Social simulation
exerciseTypes.description: Act out scenarios requiring sociolinguistic competence (a complaint, a job interview, a toast).

exerciseTypes.name: Crossword
exerciseTypes.description: Complete a crossword puzzle with lesson vocabulary.

exerciseTypes.name: Word search
exerciseTypes.description: Find hidden words in a letter grid.

exerciseTypes.name: Board game
exerciseTypes.description: Answer questions or perform tasks when landing on board game squares.

exerciseTypes.name: Speed quiz (Kahoot-style)
exerciseTypes.description: Answer multiple-choice questions against a timer.

exerciseTypes.name: Taboo / Describe without saying
exerciseTypes.description: Describe a word without using certain forbidden words.

exerciseTypes.name: Memory / pairs
exerciseTypes.description: Match pairs of cards (word-image, question-answer, synonyms).

exerciseTypes.name: Who is who / 20 questions
exerciseTypes.description: Ask yes/no questions to guess a person or object.

exerciseTypes.name: Pasapalabra / alphabet quiz
exerciseTypes.description: Give definitions as a student says the word beginning with each letter of the alphabet.
